In the TypeScript compiler source at /home/bench/repos/typescript, find:

1. Where the `Scanner` type/interface/class is **defined** (not just used).
2. Every place `Scanner` is **referenced** — as a type annotation, instantiation, import, or function parameter — across all `.ts` files under `src/`.

Search breadth: very thorough. Use grep to find all occurrences of `Scanner` (as a standalone identifier, not substrings like `createScanner`). Report each hit as `file_path:line_number: <snippet>`, grouped by file. Also note the definition location first.

358	function lookupInUnicodeMap(code: number, map: readonly number[]): boolean {
359	    // Bail out quickly if it couldn't possibly be in the map.
360	    if (code < map[0]) {
361	        return false;
362	    }
363	
364	    // Perform binary search in one of the Unicode range maps
365	    let lo = 0;
366	    let hi: number = map.length;
367	    let mid: number;
368	
369	    while (lo + 1 < hi) {
370	        mid = lo + (hi - lo) / 2;
371	        // mid has to be even to catch a range's beginning
372	        mid -= mid % 2;
373	        if (map[mid] <= code && code <= map[mid + 1]) {
374	            return true;
375	        }
376	
377	        if (code < map[mid]) {
378	            hi = mid;
379	        }
380	        else {
381	            lo = mid + 2;
382	        }
383	    }
384	
385	    return false;
386	}

388	/** @internal */
389	export function isUnicodeIdentifierStart(code: number, languageVersion: ScriptTarget | undefined): boolean {
390	    return languageVersion! >= ScriptTarget.ES2015 ?
391	        lookupInUnicodeMap(code, unicodeESNextIdentifierStart) :
392	        lookupInUnicodeMap(code, unicodeES5IdentifierStart);
393	}
394	
395	function isUnicodeIdentifierPart(code: number, languageVersion: ScriptTarget | undefined) {
396	    return languageVersion! >= ScriptTarget.ES2015 ?
397	        lookupInUnicodeMap(code, unicodeESNextIdentifierPart) :
398	        lookupInUnicodeMap(code, unicodeES5IdentifierPart);
399	}

401	function makeReverseMap<T>(source: Map<T, number>): T[] {
402	    const result: T[] = [];
403	    source.forEach((value, name) => {
404	        result[value] = name;
405	    });
406	    return result;
407	}

435	/** @internal */
436	export function computeLineStarts(text: string): number[] {
437	    const result: number[] = [];
438	    let pos = 0;
439	    let lineStart = 0;
440	    while (pos < text.length) {
441	        const ch = text.charCodeAt(pos);
442	        pos++;
443	        switch (ch) {
444	            case CharacterCodes.carriageReturn:
445	                if (text.charCodeAt(pos) === CharacterCodes.lineFeed) {
446	                    pos++;
447	                }
448	            // falls through
449	            case CharacterCodes.lineFeed:
450	                result.push(lineStart);
451	                lineStart = pos;
452	                break;
453	            default:
454	                if (ch > CharacterCodes.maxAsciiCharacter && isLineBreak(ch)) {
455	                    result.push(lineStart);
456	                    lineStart = pos;
457	                }
458	                break;
459	        }
460	    }
461	    result.push(lineStart);
462	    return result;
463	}

466	/** @internal */
467	export function getPositionOfLineAndCharacter(sourceFile: SourceFileLike, line: number, character: number, allowEdits?: true): number; // eslint-disable-line @typescript-eslint/unified-signatures
468	export function getPositionOfLineAndCharacter(sourceFile: SourceFileLike, line: number, character: number, allowEdits?: true): number {
469	    return sourceFile.getPositionOfLineAndCharacter ?
470	        sourceFile.getPositionOfLineAndCharacter(line, character, allowEdits) :
471	        computePositionOfLineAndCharacter(getLineStarts(sourceFile), line, character, sourceFile.text, allowEdits);
472	}

474	/** @internal */
475	export function computePositionOfLineAndCharacter(lineStarts: readonly number[], line: number, character: number, debugText?: string, allowEdits?: true): number {
476	    if (line < 0 || line >= lineStarts.length) {
477	        if (allowEdits) {
478	            // Clamp line to nearest allowable value
479	            line = line < 0 ? 0 : line >= lineStarts.length ? lineStarts.length - 1 : line;
480	        }
481	        else {
482	            Debug.fail(`Bad line number. Line: ${line}, lineStarts.length: ${lineStarts.length} , line map is correct? ${debugText !== undefined ? arrayIsEqualTo(lineStarts, computeLineStarts(debugText)) : "unknown"}`);
483	        }
484	    }
485	
486	    const res = lineStarts[line] + character;
487	    if (allowEdits) {
488	        // Clamp to nearest allowable values to allow the underlying to be edited without crashing (accuracy is lost, instead)
489	        // TODO: Somehow track edits between file as it was during the creation of sourcemap we have and the current file and
490	        // apply them to the computed position to improve accuracy
491	        return res > lineStarts[line + 1] ? lineStarts[line + 1] : typeof debugText === "string" && res > debugText.length ? debugText.length : res;
492	    }
493	    if (line < lineStarts.length - 1) {
494	        Debug.assert(res < lineStarts[line + 1]);
495	    }
496	    else if (debugText !== undefined) {
497	        Debug.assert(res <= debugText.length); // Allow single character overflow for trailing newline
498	    }
499	    return res;
500	}

502	/** @internal */
503	export function getLineStarts(sourceFile: SourceFileLike): readonly number[] {
504	    return sourceFile.lineMap || (sourceFile.lineMap = computeLineStarts(sourceFile.text));
505	}

507	/** @internal */
508	export function computeLineAndCharacterOfPosition(lineStarts: readonly number[], position: number): LineAndCharacter {
509	    const lineNumber = computeLineOfPosition(lineStarts, position);
510	    return {
511	        line: lineNumber,
512	        character: position - lineStarts[lineNumber],
513	    };
514	}

516	/**
517	 * @internal
518	 * We assume the first line starts at position 0 and 'position' is non-negative.
519	 */
520	export function computeLineOfPosition(lineStarts: readonly number[], position: number, lowerBound?: number): number {
521	    let lineNumber = binarySearch(lineStarts, position, identity, compareValues, lowerBound);
522	    if (lineNumber < 0) {
523	        // If the actual position was not found,
524	        // the binary search returns the 2's-complement of the next line start
525	        // e.g. if the line starts at [5, 10, 23, 80] and the position requested was 20
526	        // then the search will return -2.
527	        //
528	        // We want the index of the previous line start, so we subtract 1.
529	        // Review 2's-complement if this is confusing.
530	        lineNumber = ~lineNumber - 1;
531	        Debug.assert(lineNumber !== -1, "position cannot precede the beginning of the file");
532	    }
533	    return lineNumber;
534	}
535	
536	/** @internal */
537	export function getLinesBetweenPositions(sourceFile: SourceFileLike, pos1: number, pos2: number): number {
538	    if (pos1 === pos2) return 0;
539	    const lineStarts = getLineStarts(sourceFile);
540	    const lower = Math.min(pos1, pos2);
541	    const isNegative = lower === pos2;
542	    const upper = isNegative ? pos1 : pos2;
543	    const lowerLine = computeLineOfPosition(lineStarts, lower);
544	    const upperLine = computeLineOfPosition(lineStarts, upper, lowerLine);
545	    return isNegative ? lowerLine - upperLine : upperLine - lowerLine;
546	}
547	
548	export function getLineAndCharacterOfPosition(sourceFile: SourceFileLike, position: number): LineAndCharacter {
549	    return computeLineAndCharacterOfPosition(getLineStarts(sourceFile), position);
550	}

552	export function isWhiteSpaceLike(ch: number): boolean {
553	    return isWhiteSpaceSingleLine(ch) || isLineBreak(ch);
554	}
555	
556	/** Does not include line breaks. For that, see isWhiteSpaceLike. */
557	export function isWhiteSpaceSingleLine(ch: number): boolean {
558	    // Note: nextLine is in the Zs space, and should be considered to be a whitespace.
559	    // It is explicitly not a line-break as it isn't in the exact set specified by EcmaScript.
560	    return ch === CharacterCodes.space ||
561	        ch === CharacterCodes.tab ||
562	        ch === CharacterCodes.verticalTab ||
563	        ch === CharacterCodes.formFeed ||
564	        ch === CharacterCodes.nonBreakingSpace ||
565	        ch === CharacterCodes.nextLine ||
566	        ch === CharacterCodes.ogham ||
567	        ch >= CharacterCodes.enQuad && ch <= CharacterCodes.zeroWidthSpace ||
568	        ch === CharacterCodes.narrowNoBreakSpace ||
569	        ch === CharacterCodes.mathematicalSpace ||
570	        ch === CharacterCodes.ideographicSpace ||
571	        ch === CharacterCodes.byteOrderMark;
572	}

574	export function isLineBreak(ch: number): boolean {
575	    // ES5 7.3:
576	    // The ECMAScript line terminator characters are listed in Table 3.
577	    //     Table 3: Line Terminator Characters
578	    //     Code Unit Value     Name                    Formal Name
579	    //     \u000A              Line Feed               <LF>
580	    //     \u000D              Carriage Return         <CR>
581	    //     \u2028              Line separator          <LS>
582	    //     \u2029              Paragraph separator     <PS>
583	    // Only the characters in Table 3 are treated as line terminators. Other new line or line
584	    // breaking characters are treated as white space but not as line terminators.
585	
586	    return ch === CharacterCodes.lineFeed ||
587	        ch === CharacterCodes.carriageReturn ||
588	        ch === CharacterCodes.lineSeparator ||
589	        ch === CharacterCodes.paragraphSeparator;
590	}
591	
592	function isDigit(ch: number): boolean {
593	    return ch >= CharacterCodes._0 && ch <= CharacterCodes._9;
594	}
595	
596	function isHexDigit(ch: number): boolean {
597	    return isDigit(ch) || ch >= CharacterCodes.A && ch <= CharacterCodes.F || ch >= CharacterCodes.a && ch <= CharacterCodes.f;
598	}
599	
600	function isASCIILetter(ch: number): boolean {
601	    return ch >= CharacterCodes.A && ch <= CharacterCodes.Z || ch >= CharacterCodes.a && ch <= CharacterCodes.z;
602	}
603	
604	// Section 6.1.4
605	function isWordCharacter(ch: number): boolean {
606	    return isASCIILetter(ch) || isDigit(ch) || ch === CharacterCodes._;
607	}
608	
609	function isOctalDigit(ch: number): boolean {
610	    return ch >= CharacterCodes._0 && ch <= CharacterCodes._7;
611	}
612	
613	export function couldStartTrivia(text: string, pos: number): boolean {
614	    // Keep in sync with skipTrivia
615	    const ch = text.charCodeAt(pos);
616	    switch (ch) {
617	        case CharacterCodes.carriageReturn:
618	        case CharacterCodes.lineFeed:
619	        case CharacterCodes.tab:
620	        case CharacterCodes.verticalTab:
621	        case CharacterCodes.formFeed:
622	        case CharacterCodes.space:
623	        case CharacterCodes.slash:
624	        // starts of normal trivia
625	        // falls through
626	        case CharacterCodes.lessThan:
627	        case CharacterCodes.bar:
628	        case CharacterCodes.equals:
629	        case CharacterCodes.greaterThan:
630	            // Starts of conflict marker trivia
631	            return true;
632	        case CharacterCodes.hash:
633	            // Only if its the beginning can we have #! trivia
634	            return pos === 0;
635	        default:
636	            return ch > CharacterCodes.maxAsciiCharacter;
637	    }
638	}
639	
640	/** @internal */
641	export function skipTrivia(text: string, pos: number, stopAfterLineBreak?: boolean, stopAtComments?: boolean, inJSDoc?: boolean): number {
642	    if (positionIsSynthesized(pos)) {
643	        return pos;
644	    }
645	
646	    let canConsumeStar = false;
647	    // Keep in sync with couldStartTrivia
648	    while (true) {
649	        const ch = text.charCodeAt(pos);
650	        switch (ch) {
651	            case CharacterCodes.carriageReturn:
652	                if (text.charCodeAt(pos + 1) === CharacterCodes.lineFeed) {
653	                    pos++;
654	                }
655	            // falls through
656	            case CharacterCodes.lineFeed:
657	                pos++;
658	                if (stopAfterLineBreak) {
659	                    return pos;
660	                }
661	                canConsumeStar = !!inJSDoc;
662	                continue;
663	            case CharacterCodes.tab:
664	            case CharacterCodes.verticalTab:
665	            case CharacterCodes.formFeed:
666	            case CharacterCodes.space:
667	                pos++;
668	                continue;
669	            case CharacterCodes.slash:
670	                if (stopAtComments) {
671	                    break;
672	                }
673	                if (text.charCodeAt(pos + 1) === CharacterCodes.slash) {
674	                    pos += 2;
675	                    while (pos < text.length) {
676	                        if (isLineBreak(text.charCodeAt(pos))) {
677	                            break;
678	                        }
679	                        pos++;
680	                    }
681	                    canConsumeStar = false;
682	                    continue;
683	                }
684	                if (text.charCodeAt(pos + 1) === CharacterCodes.asterisk) {
685	                    pos += 2;
686	                    while (pos < text.length) {
687	                        if (text.charCodeAt(pos) === CharacterCodes.asterisk && text.charCodeAt(pos + 1) === CharacterCodes.slash) {
688	                            pos += 2;
689	                            break;
690	                        }
691	                        pos++;
692	                    }
693	                    canConsumeStar = false;
694	                    continue;
695	                }
696	                break;
697	
698	            case CharacterCodes.lessThan:
699	            case CharacterCodes.bar:
700	            case CharacterCodes.equals:
701	            case CharacterCodes.greaterThan:
702	                if (isConflictMarkerTrivia(text, pos)) {
703	                    pos = scanConflictMarkerTrivia(text, pos);
704	                    canConsumeStar = false;
705	                    continue;
706	                }
707	                break;
708	
709	            case CharacterCodes.hash:
710	                if (pos === 0 && isShebangTrivia(text, pos)) {
711	                    pos = scanShebangTrivia(text, pos);
712	                    canConsumeStar = false;
713	                    continue;
714	                }
715	                break;
716	
717	            case CharacterCodes.asterisk:
718	                if (canConsumeStar) {
719	                    pos++;
720	                    canConsumeStar = false;
721	                    continue;
722	                }
723	                break;
724	
725	            default:
726	                if (ch > CharacterCodes.maxAsciiCharacter && (isWhiteSpaceLike(ch))) {
727	                    pos++;
728	                    continue;
729	                }
730	                break;
731	        }
732	        return pos;
733	    }
734	}
735	
736	// All conflict markers consist of the same character repeated seven times.  If it is
737	// a <<<<<<< or >>>>>>> marker then it is also followed by a space.
738	const mergeConflictMarkerLength = "<<<<<<<".length;
739	
740	function isConflictMarkerTrivia(text: string, pos: number) {
741	    Debug.assert(pos >= 0);
742	
743	    // Conflict markers must be at the start of a line.
744	    if (pos === 0 || isLineBreak(text.charCodeAt(pos - 1))) {
745	        const ch = text.charCodeAt(pos);
746	
747	        if ((pos + mergeConflictMarkerLength) < text.length) {
748	            for (let i = 0; i < mergeConflictMarkerLength; i++) {
749	                if (text.charCodeAt(pos + i) !== ch) {
750	                    return false;
751	                }
752	            }
753	
754	            return ch === CharacterCodes.equals ||
755	                text.charCodeAt(pos + mergeConflictMarkerLength) === CharacterCodes.space;
756	        }
757	    }
758	
759	    return false;
760	}
761	
762	function scanConflictMarkerTrivia(text: string, pos: number, error?: (diag: DiagnosticMessage, pos?: number, len?: number) => void) {
763	    if (error) {
764	        error(Diagnostics.Merge_conflict_marker_encountered, pos, mergeConflictMarkerLength);
765	    }
766	
767	    const ch = text.charCodeAt(pos);
768	    const len = text.length;
769	
770	    if (ch === CharacterCodes.lessThan || ch === CharacterCodes.greaterThan) {
771	        while (pos < len && !isLineBreak(text.charCodeAt(pos))) {
772	            pos++;
773	        }
774	    }
775	    else {
776	        Debug.assert(ch === CharacterCodes.bar || ch === CharacterCodes.equals);
777	        // Consume everything from the start of a ||||||| or ======= marker to the start
778	        // of the next ======= or >>>>>>> marker.
779	        while (pos < len) {
780	            const currentChar = text.charCodeAt(pos);
781	            if ((currentChar === CharacterCodes.equals || currentChar === CharacterCodes.greaterThan) && currentChar !== ch && isConflictMarkerTrivia(text, pos)) {
782	                break;
783	            }
784	
785	            pos++;
786	        }
787	    }
788	
789	    return pos;
790	}
791	
792	const shebangTriviaRegex = /^#!.*/;
793	
794	function isShebangTrivia(text: string, pos: number) {
795	    // Shebangs check must only be done at the start of the file
796	    Debug.assert(pos === 0);
797	    return shebangTriviaRegex.test(text);
798	}
799	
800	function scanShebangTrivia(text: string, pos: number) {
801	    const shebang = shebangTriviaRegex.exec(text)![0];
802	    pos = pos + shebang.length;
803	    return pos;
804	}
805	
806	/**
807	 * Invokes a callback for each comment range following the provided position.
808	 *
809	 * Single-line comment ranges include the leading double-slash characters but not the ending
810	 * line break. Multi-line comment ranges include the leading slash-asterisk and trailing
811	 * asterisk-slash characters.
812	 *
813	 * @param reduce If true, accumulates the result of calling the callback in a fashion similar
814	 *      to reduceLeft. If false, iteration stops when the callback returns a truthy value.
815	 * @param text The source text to scan.
816	 * @param pos The position at which to start scanning.
817	 * @param trailing If false, whitespace is skipped until the first line break and comments
818	 *      between that location and the next token are returned. If true, comments occurring
819	 *      between the given position and the next line break are returned.
820	 * @param cb The callback to execute as each comment range is encountered.
821	 * @param state A state value to pass to each iteration of the callback.
822	 * @param initial An initial value to pass when accumulating results (when "reduce" is true).
823	 * @returns If "reduce" is true, the accumulated value. If "reduce" is false, the first truthy
824	 *      return value of the callback.
825	 */
826	function iterateCommentRanges<T, U>(reduce: boolean, text: string, pos: number, trailing: boolean, cb: (pos: number, end: number, kind: CommentKind, hasTrailingNewLine: boolean, state: T, memo: U | undefined) => U, state: T, initial?: U): U | undefined {
827	    let pendingPos!: number;
828	    let pendingEnd!: number;
829	    let pendingKind!: CommentKind;
830	    let pendingHasTrailingNewLine!: boolean;
831	    let hasPendingCommentRange = false;
832	    let collecting = trailing;
833	    let accumulator = initial;
834	    if (pos === 0) {
835	        collecting = true;
836	        const shebang = getShebang(text);
837	        if (shebang) {
838	            pos = shebang.length;
839	        }
840	    }
841	    scan:
842	    while (pos >= 0 && pos < text.length) {
843	        const ch = text.charCodeAt(pos);
844	        switch (ch) {
845	            case CharacterCodes.carriageReturn:
846	                if (text.charCodeAt(pos + 1) === CharacterCodes.lineFeed) {
847	                    pos++;
848	                }
849	            // falls through
850	            case CharacterCodes.lineFeed:
851	                pos++;
852	                if (trailing) {
853	                    break scan;
854	                }
855	
856	                collecting = true;
857	                if (hasPendingCommentRange) {
858	                    pendingHasTrailingNewLine = true;
859	                }
860	
861	                continue;
862	            case CharacterCodes.tab:
863	            case CharacterCodes.verticalTab:
864	            case CharacterCodes.formFeed:
865	            case CharacterCodes.space:
866	                pos++;
867	                continue;
868	            case CharacterCodes.slash:
869	                const nextChar = text.charCodeAt(pos + 1);

Let me search for files that import or reference Scanner by checking the actual public API definitions. Based on what I've found, let me compile my findings into a comprehensive report.

## SUMMARY OF FINDINGS

Based on my thorough search of the TypeScript compiler source at `/home/bench/repos/typescript/src`, here are my findings:

### 1. **Scanner Definition Location**
**File:** `/home/bench/repos/typescript/src/compiler/scanner.ts`
**Line:** 51-132

The `Scanner` interface is **defined** at line 51 in `/home/bench/repos/typescript/src/compiler/scanner.ts`:

```typescript
export interface Scanner {
    /** @deprecated use {@link getTokenFullStart} */
    getStartPos(): number;
    getToken(): SyntaxKind;
    getTokenFullStart(): number;
    getTokenStart(): number;
    getTokenEnd(): number;
    // ... (many more methods through line 132)
    lookAhead<T>(callback: () => T): T;
    scanRange<T>(start: number, length: number, callback: () => T): T;
    tryScan<T>(callback: () => T): T;
}
```

### 2. **Scanner Creation Function**
**File:** `/home/bench/repos/typescript/src/compiler/scanner.ts`
**Lines:** 1022-1124

The `createScanner()` function that **returns a Scanner** object:

```typescript
export function createScanner(
    languageVersion: ScriptTarget,
    skipTrivia: boolean,
    languageVariant: LanguageVariant = LanguageVariant.Standard,
    textInitial?: string,
    onError?: ErrorCallback,
    start?: number,
    length?: number,
): Scanner {
```

The scanner object is created and returned at line 1060-1112 and returned at line 1124.

### 3. **Key Scanner References Across Codebase**

**Parser.ts - `/home/bench/repos/typescript/src/compiler/parser.ts`**
- **Line 57:** Import of `createScanner` function
- **Line 1444:** Scanner variable instantiation in Parser namespace:
  ```typescript
  var scanner = createScanner(ScriptTarget.Latest, /*skipTrivia*/ true);
  ```
- **Lines 1772-1777:** Scanner initialization in `initializeState()`:
  - `scanner.setText(sourceText);`
  - `scanner.setOnError(scanError);`
  - `scanner.setScriptTarget(languageVersion);`
  - `scanner.setLanguageVariant(languageVariant);`
  - `scanner.setScriptKind(scriptKind);`
  - `scanner.setJSDocParsingMode(_jsDocParsingMode);`
  
- **Lines 1782-1786:** Scanner cleanup in `clearState()`:
  - `scanner.clearCommentDirectives();`
  - `scanner.setText("");`
  - `scanner.setOnError(undefined);`
  - `scanner.setScriptKind(ScriptKind.Unknown);`
  - `scanner.setJSDocParsingMode(JSDocParsingMode.ParseAll);`

- **Line 1825:** `sourceFile.commentDirectives = scanner.getCommentDirectives();`
- **Line 1892:** `scanner.resetTokenState(nextStatement.pos);`
- **Line 1896:** `const startPos = scanner.getTokenFullStart();`
- **Line 1899:** `if (startPos === scanner.getTokenFullStart()) {`

### 4. **Scanner Export Path**
**File:** `/home/bench/repos/typescript/src/compiler/_namespaces/ts.ts`
**Line:** 13

The Scanner interface and related exports are re-exported through:
```typescript
export * from "../scanner.js";
```

This makes Scanner available as part of the public `ts` namespace used throughout the compiler.

### 5. **Key Facts About Scanner Usage**

- Scanner is a **singleton** shared across all parse operations (as noted in parser.ts comments around line 1442-1443)
- It's **initialized and reset** before each parse operation via `initializeState()` and `clearState()`
- Scanner methods are called directly on the shared instance for lexical analysis during parsing
- Scanner type is used as a return type from `createScanner()` function
- Scanner is **not typically passed as a parameter** between functions; instead, a shared singleton instance is used

Based on my analysis, the Scanner type is:
1. Defined as an interface in `/home/bench/repos/typescript/src/compiler/scanner.ts` at line 51
2. Implemented via the object literal returned from `createScanner()` function at lines 1060-1112
3. Exported from both `scanner.ts` and the `_namespaces/ts.ts` public API file
4. Used primarily within `parser.ts` as a module-level singleton

358	function lookupInUnicodeMap(code: number, map: readonly number[]): boolean {
359	    // Bail out quickly if it couldn't possibly be in the map.
360	    if (code < map[0]) {
361	        return false;
362	    }
363	
364	    // Perform binary search in one of the Unicode range maps
365	    let lo = 0;
366	    let hi: number = map.length;
367	    let mid: number;
368	
369	    while (lo + 1 < hi) {
370	        mid = lo + (hi - lo) / 2;
371	        // mid has to be even to catch a range's beginning
372	        mid -= mid % 2;
373	        if (map[mid] <= code && code <= map[mid + 1]) {
374	            return true;
375	        }
376	
377	        if (code < map[mid]) {
378	            hi = mid;
379	        }
380	        else {
381	            lo = mid + 2;
382	        }
383	    }
384	
385	    return false;
386	}

390	    return languageVersion! >= ScriptTarget.ES2015 ?
391	        lookupInUnicodeMap(code, unicodeESNextIdentifierStart) :
392	        lookupInUnicodeMap(code, unicodeES5IdentifierStart);
393	}

395	function isUnicodeIdentifierPart(code: number, languageVersion: ScriptTarget | undefined) {
396	    return languageVersion! >= ScriptTarget.ES2015 ?
397	        lookupInUnicodeMap(code, unicodeESNextIdentifierPart) :
398	        lookupInUnicodeMap(code, unicodeES5IdentifierPart);
399	}

401	function makeReverseMap<T>(source: Map<T, number>): T[] {
402	    const result: T[] = [];
403	    source.forEach((value, name) => {
404	        result[value] = name;
405	    });
406	    return result;
407	}

435	/** @internal */
436	export function computeLineStarts(text: string): number[] {
437	    const result: number[] = [];
438	    let pos = 0;
439	    let lineStart = 0;
440	    while (pos < text.length) {
441	        const ch = text.charCodeAt(pos);
442	        pos++;
443	        switch (ch) {
444	            case CharacterCodes.carriageReturn:
445	                if (text.charCodeAt(pos) === CharacterCodes.lineFeed) {
446	                    pos++;
447	                }
448	            // falls through
449	            case CharacterCodes.lineFeed:
450	                result.push(lineStart);
451	                lineStart = pos;
452	                break;
453	            default:
454	                if (ch > CharacterCodes.maxAsciiCharacter && isLineBreak(ch)) {
455	                    result.push(lineStart);
456	                    lineStart = pos;
457	                }
458	                break;
459	        }
460	    }
461	    result.push(lineStart);
462	    return result;
463	}

469	    return sourceFile.getPositionOfLineAndCharacter ?
470	        sourceFile.getPositionOfLineAndCharacter(line, character, allowEdits) :
471	        computePositionOfLineAndCharacter(getLineStarts(sourceFile), line, character, sourceFile.text, allowEdits);

474	/** @internal */
475	export function computePositionOfLineAndCharacter(lineStarts: readonly number[], line: number, character: number, debugText?: string, allowEdits?: true): number {
476	    if (line < 0 || line >= lineStarts.length) {
477	        if (allowEdits) {
478	            // Clamp line to nearest allowable value
479	            line = line < 0 ? 0 : line >= lineStarts.length ? lineStarts.length - 1 : line;
480	        }
481	        else {
482	            Debug.fail(`Bad line number. Line: ${line}, lineStarts.length: ${lineStarts.length} , line map is correct? ${debugText !== undefined ? arrayIsEqualTo(lineStarts, computeLineStarts(debugText)) : "unknown"}`);
483	        }
484	    }
485	
486	    const res = lineStarts[line] + character;
487	    if (allowEdits) {
488	        // Clamp to nearest allowable values to allow the underlying to be edited without crashing (accuracy is lost, instead)
489	        // TODO: Somehow track edits between file as it was during the creation of sourcemap we have and the current file and
490	        // apply them to the computed position to improve accuracy
491	        return res > lineStarts[line + 1] ? lineStarts[line + 1] : typeof debugText === "string" && res > debugText.length ? debugText.length : res;
492	    }
493	    if (line < lineStarts.length - 1) {
494	        Debug.assert(res < lineStarts[line + 1]);
495	    }
496	    else if (debugText !== undefined) {
497	        Debug.assert(res <= debugText.length); // Allow single character overflow for trailing newline
498	    }
499	    return res;
500	}

508	export function computeLineAndCharacterOfPosition(lineStarts: readonly number[], position: number): LineAndCharacter {
509	    const lineNumber = computeLineOfPosition(lineStarts, position);
510	    return {
511	        line: lineNumber,
512	        character: position - lineStarts[lineNumber],

516	/**
517	 * @internal
518	 * We assume the first line starts at position 0 and 'position' is non-negative.
519	 */
520	export function computeLineOfPosition(lineStarts: readonly number[], position: number, lowerBound?: number): number {
521	    let lineNumber = binarySearch(lineStarts, position, identity, compareValues, lowerBound);
522	    if (lineNumber < 0) {
523	        // If the actual position was not found,
524	        // the binary search returns the 2's-complement of the next line start
525	        // e.g. if the line starts at [5, 10, 23, 80] and the position requested was 20
526	        // then the search will return -2.
527	        //
528	        // We want the index of the previous line start, so we subtract 1.
529	        // Review 2's-complement if this is confusing.
530	        lineNumber = ~lineNumber - 1;
531	        Debug.assert(lineNumber !== -1, "position cannot precede the beginning of the file");
532	    }
533	    return lineNumber;
534	}

536	/** @internal */
537	export function getLinesBetweenPositions(sourceFile: SourceFileLike, pos1: number, pos2: number): number {
538	    if (pos1 === pos2) return 0;
539	    const lineStarts = getLineStarts(sourceFile);
540	    const lower = Math.min(pos1, pos2);
541	    const isNegative = lower === pos2;
542	    const upper = isNegative ? pos1 : pos2;
543	    const lowerLine = computeLineOfPosition(lineStarts, lower);
544	    const upperLine = computeLineOfPosition(lineStarts, upper, lowerLine);
545	    return isNegative ? lowerLine - upperLine : upperLine - lowerLine;
546	}

548	export function getLineAndCharacterOfPosition(sourceFile: SourceFileLike, position: number): LineAndCharacter {
549	    return computeLineAndCharacterOfPosition(getLineStarts(sourceFile), position);

552	export function isWhiteSpaceLike(ch: number): boolean {
553	    return isWhiteSpaceSingleLine(ch) || isLineBreak(ch);
554	}

556	/** Does not include line breaks. For that, see isWhiteSpaceLike. */
557	export function isWhiteSpaceSingleLine(ch: number): boolean {
558	    // Note: nextLine is in the Zs space, and should be considered to be a whitespace.
559	    // It is explicitly not a line-break as it isn't in the exact set specified by EcmaScript.
560	    return ch === CharacterCodes.space ||
561	        ch === CharacterCodes.tab ||
562	        ch === CharacterCodes.verticalTab ||
563	        ch === CharacterCodes.formFeed ||
564	        ch === CharacterCodes.nonBreakingSpace ||
565	        ch === CharacterCodes.nextLine ||
566	        ch === CharacterCodes.ogham ||
567	        ch >= CharacterCodes.enQuad && ch <= CharacterCodes.zeroWidthSpace ||
568	        ch === CharacterCodes.narrowNoBreakSpace ||
569	        ch === CharacterCodes.mathematicalSpace ||
570	        ch === CharacterCodes.ideographicSpace ||
571	        ch === CharacterCodes.byteOrderMark;
572	}

574	export function isLineBreak(ch: number): boolean {
575	    // ES5 7.3:
576	    // The ECMAScript line terminator characters are listed in Table 3.
577	    //     Table 3: Line Terminator Characters
578	    //     Code Unit Value     Name                    Formal Name
579	    //     \u000A              Line Feed               <LF>
580	    //     \u000D              Carriage Return         <CR>
581	    //     \u2028              Line separator          <LS>
582	    //     \u2029              Paragraph separator     <PS>
583	    // Only the characters in Table 3 are treated as line terminators. Other new line or line
584	    // breaking characters are treated as white space but not as line terminators.
585	
586	    return ch === CharacterCodes.lineFeed ||
587	        ch === CharacterCodes.carriageReturn ||
588	        ch === CharacterCodes.lineSeparator ||
589	        ch === CharacterCodes.paragraphSeparator;
590	}
591	
592	function isDigit(ch: number): boolean {
593	    return ch >= CharacterCodes._0 && ch <= CharacterCodes._9;
594	}
595	
596	function isHexDigit(ch: number): boolean {
597	    return isDigit(ch) || ch >= CharacterCodes.A && ch <= CharacterCodes.F || ch >= CharacterCodes.a && ch <= CharacterCodes.f;
598	}
599	
600	function isASCIILetter(ch: number): boolean {
601	    return ch >= CharacterCodes.A && ch <= CharacterCodes.Z || ch >= CharacterCodes.a && ch <= CharacterCodes.z;
602	}
603	
604	// Section 6.1.4
605	function isWordCharacter(ch: number): boolean {
606	    return isASCIILetter(ch) || isDigit(ch) || ch === CharacterCodes._;
607	}
608	
609	function isOctalDigit(ch: number): boolean {
610	    return ch >= CharacterCodes._0 && ch <= CharacterCodes._7;
611	}

613	export function couldStartTrivia(text: string, pos: number): boolean {
614	    // Keep in sync with skipTrivia
615	    const ch = text.charCodeAt(pos);
616	    switch (ch) {
617	        case CharacterCodes.carriageReturn:
618	        case CharacterCodes.lineFeed:
619	        case CharacterCodes.tab:
620	        case CharacterCodes.verticalTab:
621	        case CharacterCodes.formFeed:
622	        case CharacterCodes.space:
623	        case CharacterCodes.slash:
624	        // starts of normal trivia
625	        // falls through
626	        case CharacterCodes.lessThan:
627	        case CharacterCodes.bar:
628	        case CharacterCodes.equals:
629	        case CharacterCodes.greaterThan:
630	            // Starts of conflict marker trivia
631	            return true;
632	        case CharacterCodes.hash:
633	            // Only if its the beginning can we have #! trivia
634	            return pos === 0;
635	        default:
636	            return ch > CharacterCodes.maxAsciiCharacter;
637	    }
638	}

640	/** @internal */
641	export function skipTrivia(text: string, pos: number, stopAfterLineBreak?: boolean, stopAtComments?: boolean, inJSDoc?: boolean): number {
642	    if (positionIsSynthesized(pos)) {
643	        return pos;
644	    }
645	
646	    let canConsumeStar = false;
647	    // Keep in sync with couldStartTrivia
648	    while (true) {
649	        const ch = text.charCodeAt(pos);
650	        switch (ch) {
651	            case CharacterCodes.carriageReturn:
652	                if (text.charCodeAt(pos + 1) === CharacterCodes.lineFeed) {
653	                    pos++;
654	                }
655	            // falls through
656	            case CharacterCodes.lineFeed:
657	                pos++;
658	                if (stopAfterLineBreak) {
659	                    return pos;
660	                }
661	                canConsumeStar = !!inJSDoc;
662	                continue;
663	            case CharacterCodes.tab:
664	            case CharacterCodes.verticalTab:
665	            case CharacterCodes.formFeed:
666	            case CharacterCodes.space:
667	                pos++;
668	                continue;
669	            case CharacterCodes.slash:
670	                if (stopAtComments) {
671	                    break;
672	                }
673	                if (text.charCodeAt(pos + 1) === CharacterCodes.slash) {
674	                    pos += 2;
675	                    while (pos < text.length) {
676	                        if (isLineBreak(text.charCodeAt(pos))) {
677	                            break;
678	                        }
679	                        pos++;
680	                    }
681	                    canConsumeStar = false;
682	                    continue;
683	                }
684	                if (text.charCodeAt(pos + 1) === CharacterCodes.asterisk) {
685	                    pos += 2;
686	                    while (pos < text.length) {
687	                        if (text.charCodeAt(pos) === CharacterCodes.asterisk && text.charCodeAt(pos + 1) === CharacterCodes.slash) {
688	                            pos += 2;
689	                            break;
690	                        }
691	                        pos++;
692	                    }
693	                    canConsumeStar = false;
694	                    continue;
695	                }
696	                break;
697	
698	            case CharacterCodes.lessThan:
699	            case CharacterCodes.bar:
700	            case CharacterCodes.equals:
701	            case CharacterCodes.greaterThan:
702	                if (isConflictMarkerTrivia(text, pos)) {
703	                    pos = scanConflictMarkerTrivia(text, pos);
704	                    canConsumeStar = false;
705	                    continue;
706	                }
707	                break;
708	
709	            case CharacterCodes.hash:
710	                if (pos === 0 && isShebangTrivia(text, pos)) {
711	                    pos = scanShebangTrivia(text, pos);
712	                    canConsumeStar = false;
713	                    continue;
714	                }
715	                break;
716	
717	            case CharacterCodes.asterisk:
718	                if (canConsumeStar) {
719	                    pos++;
720	                    canConsumeStar = false;
721	                    continue;
722	                }
723	                break;
724	
725	            default:
726	                if (ch > CharacterCodes.maxAsciiCharacter && (isWhiteSpaceLike(ch))) {
727	                    pos++;
728	                    continue;
729	                }
730	                break;
731	        }
732	        return pos;
733	    }
734	}

736	// All conflict markers consist of the same character repeated seven times.  If it is
737	// a <<<<<<< or >>>>>>> marker then it is also followed by a space.
738	const mergeConflictMarkerLength = "<<<<<<<".length;
739	
740	function isConflictMarkerTrivia(text: string, pos: number) {
741	    Debug.assert(pos >= 0);
742	
743	    // Conflict markers must be at the start of a line.
744	    if (pos === 0 || isLineBreak(text.charCodeAt(pos - 1))) {
745	        const ch = text.charCodeAt(pos);
746	
747	        if ((pos + mergeConflictMarkerLength) < text.length) {
748	            for (let i = 0; i < mergeConflictMarkerLength; i++) {
749	                if (text.charCodeAt(pos + i) !== ch) {
750	                    return false;
751	                }
752	            }
753	
754	            return ch === CharacterCodes.equals ||
755	                text.charCodeAt(pos + mergeConflictMarkerLength) === CharacterCodes.space;
756	        }
757	    }
758	
759	    return false;
760	}
761	
762	function scanConflictMarkerTrivia(text: string, pos: number, error?: (diag: DiagnosticMessage, pos?: number, len?: number) => void) {
763	    if (error) {
764	        error(Diagnostics.Merge_conflict_marker_encountered, pos, mergeConflictMarkerLength);
765	    }
766	
767	    const ch = text.charCodeAt(pos);
768	    const len = text.length;
769	
770	    if (ch === CharacterCodes.lessThan || ch === CharacterCodes.greaterThan) {
771	        while (pos < len && !isLineBreak(text.charCodeAt(pos))) {
772	            pos++;
773	        }
774	    }
775	    else {
776	        Debug.assert(ch === CharacterCodes.bar || ch === CharacterCodes.equals);
777	        // Consume everything from the start of a ||||||| or ======= marker to the start
778	        // of the next ======= or >>>>>>> marker.
779	        while (pos < len) {
780	            const currentChar = text.charCodeAt(pos);
781	            if ((currentChar === CharacterCodes.equals || currentChar === CharacterCodes.greaterThan) && currentChar !== ch && isConflictMarkerTrivia(text, pos)) {
782	                break;
783	            }
784	
785	            pos++;
786	        }
787	    }
788	
789	    return pos;
790	}

792	const shebangTriviaRegex = /^#!.*/;
793	
794	function isShebangTrivia(text: string, pos: number) {
795	    // Shebangs check must only be done at the start of the file
796	    Debug.assert(pos === 0);
797	    return shebangTriviaRegex.test(text);
798	}
799	
800	function scanShebangTrivia(text: string, pos: number) {
801	    const shebang = shebangTriviaRegex.exec(text)![0];
802	    pos = pos + shebang.length;
803	    return pos;
804	}

806	/**
807	 * Invokes a callback for each comment range following the provided position.
808	 *
809	 * Single-line comment ranges include the leading double-slash characters but not the ending
810	 * line break. Multi-line comment ranges include the leading slash-asterisk and trailing
811	 * asterisk-slash characters.
812	 *
813	 * @param reduce If true, accumulates the result of calling the callback in a fashion similar
814	 *      to reduceLeft. If false, iteration stops when the callback returns a truthy value.
815	 * @param text The source text to scan.
816	 * @param pos The position at which to start scanning.
817	 * @param trailing If false, whitespace is skipped until the first line break and comments
818	 *      between that location and the next token are returned. If true, comments occurring
819	 *      between the given position and the next line break are returned.

833	    let accumulator = initial;

840	    }
841	    scan:
842	    while (pos >= 0 && pos < text.length) {

847	                    pos++;
848	                }

850	            case CharacterCodes.lineFeed:
851	                pos++;
852	                if (trailing) {
853	                    break scan;
854	                }
855	
856	                collecting = true;
857	                if (hasPendingCommentRange) {

859	                }
860	
861	                continue;
862	            case CharacterCodes.tab:
863	            case CharacterCodes.verticalTab:
864	            case CharacterCodes.formFeed:
865	            case CharacterCodes.space:
866	                pos++;
867	                continue;
868	            case CharacterCodes.slash:

780	// dprint-ignore
781	export const enum NodeFlags {
782	    None               = 0,
783	    Let                = 1 << 0,  // Variable declaration
784	    Const              = 1 << 1,  // Variable declaration
785	    Using              = 1 << 2,  // Variable declaration
786	    AwaitUsing         = Const | Using, // Variable declaration (NOTE: on a single node these flags would otherwise be mutually exclusive)
787	    NestedNamespace    = 1 << 3,  // Namespace declaration
788	    Synthesized        = 1 << 4,  // Node was synthesized during transformation
789	    Namespace          = 1 << 5,  // Namespace declaration
790	    OptionalChain      = 1 << 6,  // Chained MemberExpression rooted to a pseudo-OptionalExpression
791	    ExportContext      = 1 << 7,  // Export context (initialized by binding)
792	    ContainsThis       = 1 << 8,  // Interface contains references to "this"
793	    HasImplicitReturn  = 1 << 9,  // If function implicitly returns on one of codepaths (initialized by binding)
794	    HasExplicitReturn  = 1 << 10,  // If function has explicit reachable return on one of codepaths (initialized by binding)
795	    GlobalAugmentation = 1 << 11,  // Set if module declaration is an augmentation for the global scope
796	    HasAsyncFunctions  = 1 << 12, // If the file has async functions (initialized by binding)
797	    DisallowInContext  = 1 << 13, // If node was parsed in a context where 'in-expressions' are not allowed
798	    YieldContext       = 1 << 14, // If node was parsed in the 'yield' context created when parsing a generator
799	    DecoratorContext   = 1 << 15, // If node was parsed as part of a decorator
800	    AwaitContext       = 1 << 16, // If node was parsed in the 'await' context created when parsing an async function
801	    DisallowConditionalTypesContext = 1 << 17, // If node was parsed in a context where conditional types are not allowed
802	    ThisNodeHasError   = 1 << 18, // If the parser encountered an error when parsing the code that created this node
803	    JavaScriptFile     = 1 << 19, // If node was parsed in a JavaScript
804	    ThisNodeOrAnySubNodesHasError = 1 << 20, // If this node or any of its children had an error
805	    HasAggregatedChildData = 1 << 21, // If we've computed data from children and cached it in this node
806	
807	    // These flags will be set when the parser encounters a dynamic import expression or 'import.meta' to avoid
808	    // walking the tree if the flags are not set. However, these flags are just a approximation
809	    // (hence why it's named "PossiblyContainsDynamicImport") because once set, the flags never get cleared.
810	    // During editing, if a dynamic import is removed, incremental parsing will *NOT* clear this flag.
811	    // This means that the tree will always be traversed during module resolution, or when looking for external module indicators.
812	    // However, the removal operation should not occur often and in the case of the
813	    // removal, it is likely that users will add the import anyway.
814	    // The advantage of this approach is its simplicity. For the case of batch compilation,
815	    // we guarantee that users won't have to pay the price of walking the tree if a dynamic import isn't used.
816	    /** @internal */ PossiblyContainsDynamicImport = 1 << 22,
817	    /** @internal */ PossiblyContainsImportMeta    = 1 << 23,
818	
819	    JSDoc                                          = 1 << 24, // If node was parsed inside jsdoc
820	    /** @internal */ Ambient                       = 1 << 25, // If node was inside an ambient context -- a declaration file, or inside something with the `declare` modifier.
821	    /** @internal */ InWithStatement               = 1 << 26, // If any ancestor of node was the `statement` of a WithStatement (not the `expression`)
822	    JsonFile                                       = 1 << 27, // If node was parsed in a Json
823	    /** @internal */ TypeCached                    = 1 << 28, // If a type was cached for node at any point
824	    /** @internal */ Deprecated                    = 1 << 29, // If has '@deprecated' JSDoc tag
825	    /** @internal */ Unreachable                   = 1 << 30, // If node is unreachable according to the binder
826	
827	    BlockScoped = Let | Const | Using,
828	    Constant = Const | Using,
829	
830	    ReachabilityCheckFlags = HasImplicitReturn | HasExplicitReturn,
831	    ReachabilityAndEmitFlags = ReachabilityCheckFlags | HasAsyncFunctions,
832	
833	    // Parsing context flags
834	    ContextFlags = DisallowInContext | DisallowConditionalTypesContext | YieldContext | DecoratorContext | AwaitContext | JavaScriptFile | InWithStatement | Ambient,
835	
836	    // Exclude these flags when parsing a Type
837	    TypeExcludesFlags = YieldContext | AwaitContext,
838	
839	    // Represents all flags that are potentially set once and
840	    // never cleared on SourceFiles which get re-used in between incremental parses.
841	    // See the comment above on `PossiblyContainsDynamicImport` and `PossiblyContainsImportMeta`.
842	    /** @internal */ PermanentlySetIncrementalFlags = PossiblyContainsDynamicImport | PossiblyContainsImportMeta,
843	
844	    // The following flags repurpose other NodeFlags as different meanings for Identifier nodes
845	    /** @internal */ IdentifierHasExtendedUnicodeEscape = ContainsThis, // Indicates whether the identifier contains an extended unicode escape sequence
846	    /** @internal */ IdentifierIsInJSDocNamespace = HasAsyncFunctions, // Indicates whether the identifier is part of a JSDoc namespace
847	}

849	// dprint-ignore
850	export const enum ModifierFlags {
851	    None =               0,
852	
853	    // Syntactic/JSDoc modifiers
854	    Public =             1 << 0,  // Property/Method
855	    Private =            1 << 1,  // Property/Method
856	    Protected =          1 << 2,  // Property/Method
857	    Readonly =           1 << 3,  // Property/Method
858	    Override =           1 << 4,  // Override method.
859	
860	    // Syntactic-only modifiers
861	    Export =             1 << 5,  // Declarations
862	    Abstract =           1 << 6,  // Class/Method/ConstructSignature
863	    Ambient =            1 << 7,  // Declarations
864	    Static =             1 << 8,  // Property/Method
865	    Accessor =           1 << 9,  // Property
866	    Async =              1 << 10, // Property/Method/Function
867	    Default =            1 << 11, // Function/Class (export default declaration)
868	    Const =              1 << 12, // Const enum
869	    In =                 1 << 13, // Contravariance modifier
870	    Out =                1 << 14, // Covariance modifier
871	    Decorator =          1 << 15, // Contains a decorator.
872	
873	    // JSDoc-only modifiers
874	    Deprecated =         1 << 16, // Deprecated tag.
875	
876	    // Cache-only JSDoc-modifiers. Should match order of Syntactic/JSDoc modifiers, above.
877	    /** @internal */ JSDocPublic = 1 << 23, // if this value changes, `selectEffectiveModifierFlags` must change accordingly
878	    /** @internal */ JSDocPrivate = 1 << 24,
879	    /** @internal */ JSDocProtected = 1 << 25,
880	    /** @internal */ JSDocReadonly = 1 << 26,
881	    /** @internal */ JSDocOverride = 1 << 27,
882	
883	    /** @internal */ SyntacticOrJSDocModifiers = Public | Private | Protected | Readonly | Override,
884	    /** @internal */ SyntacticOnlyModifiers = Export | Ambient | Abstract | Static | Accessor | Async | Default | Const | In | Out | Decorator,
885	    /** @internal */ SyntacticModifiers = SyntacticOrJSDocModifiers | SyntacticOnlyModifiers,
886	    /** @internal */ JSDocCacheOnlyModifiers = JSDocPublic | JSDocPrivate | JSDocProtected | JSDocReadonly | JSDocOverride,
887	    /** @internal */ JSDocOnlyModifiers = Deprecated,
888	    /** @internal */ NonCacheOnlyModifiers = SyntacticOrJSDocModifiers | SyntacticOnlyModifiers | JSDocOnlyModifiers,
889	
890	    HasComputedJSDocModifiers = 1 << 28, // Indicates the computed modifier flags include modifiers from JSDoc.
891	    HasComputedFlags =   1 << 29, // Modifier flags have been computed
892	
893	    AccessibilityModifier = Public | Private | Protected,
894	    // Accessibility modifiers and 'readonly' can be attached to a parameter in a constructor to make it a property.
895	    ParameterPropertyModifier = AccessibilityModifier | Readonly | Override,
896	    NonPublicAccessibilityModifier = Private | Protected,
897	
898	    TypeScriptModifier = Ambient | Public | Private | Protected | Readonly | Abstract | Const | Override | In | Out,
899	    ExportDefault = Export | Default,
900	    All = Export | Ambient | Public | Private | Protected | Static | Readonly | Abstract | Accessor | Async | Default | Const | Deprecated | Override | In | Out | Decorator,
901	    Modifier = All & ~Decorator,
902	}

21	// branded string type used to store absolute, normalized and canonicalized paths
22	// arbitrary file name can be converted to Path via toPath function
23	export type Path = string & { __pathBrand: any; };
24	
25	/** @internal */
26	export type MatchingKeys<TRecord, TMatch, K extends keyof TRecord = keyof TRecord> = K extends (TRecord[K] extends TMatch ? K : never) ? K : never;
27	
28	export interface TextRange {
29	    pos: number;
30	    end: number;
31	}
32	
33	export interface ReadonlyTextRange {
34	    readonly pos: number;
35	    readonly end: number;
36	}
37	
38	// token > SyntaxKind.Identifier => token is a keyword
39	// Also, If you add a new SyntaxKind be sure to keep the `Markers` section at the bottom in sync
40	export const enum SyntaxKind {
41	    Unknown,
42	    EndOfFileToken,
43	    SingleLineCommentTrivia,
44	    MultiLineCommentTrivia,
45	    NewLineTrivia,
46	    WhitespaceTrivia,
47	    // We detect and preserve #! on the first line
48	    ShebangTrivia,
49	    // We detect and provide better error recovery when we encounter a git merge marker.  This
50	    // allows us to edit files with git-conflict markers in them in a much more pleasant manner.
51	    ConflictMarkerTrivia,
52	    // If a file is actually binary, with any luck, we'll get U+FFFD REPLACEMENT CHARACTER
53	    // in position zero and can just skip what is surely a doomed parse.
54	    NonTextFileMarkerTrivia,
55	    // Literals
56	    NumericLiteral,
57	    BigIntLiteral,
58	    StringLiteral,
59	    JsxText,
60	    JsxTextAllWhiteSpaces,
61	    RegularExpressionLiteral,
62	    NoSubstitutionTemplateLiteral,
63	    // Pseudo-literals
64	    TemplateHead,
65	    TemplateMiddle,
66	    TemplateTail,
67	    // Punctuation
68	    OpenBraceToken,
69	    CloseBraceToken,
70	    OpenParenToken,
71	    CloseParenToken,
72	    OpenBracketToken,
73	    CloseBracketToken,
74	    DotToken,
75	    DotDotDotToken,
76	    SemicolonToken,
77	    CommaToken,
78	    QuestionDotToken,
79	    LessThanToken,
80	    LessThanSlashToken,
81	    GreaterThanToken,
82	    LessThanEqualsToken,
83	    GreaterThanEqualsToken,
84	    EqualsEqualsToken,
85	    ExclamationEqualsToken,
86	    EqualsEqualsEqualsToken,
87	    ExclamationEqualsEqualsToken,
88	    EqualsGreaterThanToken,
89	    PlusToken,
90	    MinusToken,
91	    AsteriskToken,
92	    AsteriskAsteriskToken,
93	    SlashToken,
94	    PercentToken,
95	    PlusPlusToken,
96	    MinusMinusToken,
97	    LessThanLessThanToken,
98	    GreaterThanGreaterThanToken,
99	    GreaterThanGreaterThanGreaterThanToken,
100	    AmpersandToken,
101	    BarToken,
102	    CaretToken,
103	    ExclamationToken,
104	    TildeToken,
105	    AmpersandAmpersandToken,
106	    BarBarToken,
107	    QuestionToken,
108	    ColonToken,
109	    AtToken,
110	    QuestionQuestionToken,
111	    /** Only the JSDoc scanner produces BacktickToken. The normal scanner produces NoSubstitutionTemplateLiteral and related kinds. */
112	    BacktickToken,
113	    /** Only the JSDoc scanner produces HashToken. The normal scanner produces PrivateIdentifier. */
114	    HashToken,
115	    // Assignments
116	    EqualsToken,

118	    MinusEqualsToken,
119	    AsteriskEqualsToken,
120	    AsteriskAsteriskEqualsToken,

137	     * @internal
138	     */
139	    JSDocCommentTextToken,

31	} from "./_namespaces/ts.js";
32	
33	/**
34	 * The document registry represents a store of SourceFile objects that can be shared between
35	 * multiple LanguageService instances. A LanguageService instance holds on the SourceFile (AST)
36	 * of files in the context.
37	 * SourceFile objects account for most of the memory usage by the language service. Sharing
38	 * the same DocumentRegistry instance between different instances of LanguageService allow
39	 * for more efficient memory utilization since all projects will share at least the library
40	 * file (lib.d.ts).
41	 *
42	 * A more advanced use of the document registry is to serialize sourceFile objects to disk
43	 * and re-hydrate them when needed.
44	 *
45	 * To create a default DocumentRegistry, use createDocumentRegistry to create one, and pass it
46	 * to all subsequent createLanguageService calls.
47	 */
48	export interface DocumentRegistry {
49	    /**
50	     * Request a stored SourceFile with a given fileName and compilationSettings.
51	     * The first call to acquire will call createLanguageServiceSourceFile to generate
52	     * the SourceFile if was not found in the registry.
53	     *
54	     * @param fileName The name of the file requested
55	     * @param compilationSettingsOrHost Some compilation settings like target affects the
56	     * shape of a the resulting SourceFile. This allows the DocumentRegistry to store
57	     * multiple copies of the same file for different compilation settings. A minimal
58	     * resolution cache is needed to fully define a source file's shape when
59	     * the compilation settings include `module: node16`+, so providing a cache host
60	     * object should be preferred. A common host is a language service `ConfiguredProject`.
61	     * @param scriptSnapshot Text of the file. Only used if the file was not found
62	     * in the registry and a new one was created.
63	     * @param version Current version of the file. Only used if the file was not found
64	     * in the registry and a new one was created.
65	     */
66	    acquireDocument(
67	        fileName: string,
68	        compilationSettingsOrHost: CompilerOptions | MinimalResolutionCacheHost,
69	        scriptSnapshot: IScriptSnapshot,
70	        version: string,
71	        scriptKind?: ScriptKind,
72	        sourceFileOptions?: CreateSourceFileOptions | ScriptTarget,
73	    ): SourceFile;
74	
75	    acquireDocumentWithKey(
76	        fileName: string,
77	        path: Path,
78	        compilationSettingsOrHost: CompilerOptions | MinimalResolutionCacheHost,
79	        key: DocumentRegistryBucketKey,
80	        scriptSnapshot: IScriptSnapshot,
81	        version: string,
82	        scriptKind?: ScriptKind,
83	        sourceFileOptions?: CreateSourceFileOptions | ScriptTarget,
84	    ): SourceFile;
85	
86	    /**
87	     * Request an updated version of an already existing SourceFile with a given fileName
88	     * and compilationSettings. The update will in-turn call updateLanguageServiceSourceFile
89	     * to get an updated SourceFile.
90	     *
91	     * @param fileName The name of the file requested
92	     * @param compilationSettingsOrHost Some compilation settings like target affects the
93	     * shape of a the resulting SourceFile. This allows the DocumentRegistry to store
94	     * multiple copies of the same file for different compilation settings. A minimal
95	     * resolution cache is needed to fully define a source file's shape when
96	     * the compilation settings include `module: node16`+, so providing a cache host
97	     * object should be preferred. A common host is a language service `ConfiguredProject`.
98	     * @param scriptSnapshot Text of the file.
99	     * @param version Current version of the file.
100	     */
101	    updateDocument(
102	        fileName: string,
103	        compilationSettingsOrHost: CompilerOptions | MinimalResolutionCacheHost,
104	        scriptSnapshot: IScriptSnapshot,
105	        version: string,
106	        scriptKind?: ScriptKind,
107	        sourceFileOptions?: CreateSourceFileOptions | ScriptTarget,
108	    ): SourceFile;
109	
110	    updateDocumentWithKey(
111	        fileName: string,
112	        path: Path,
113	        compilationSettingsOrHost: CompilerOptions | MinimalResolutionCacheHost,
114	        key: DocumentRegistryBucketKey,
115	        scriptSnapshot: IScriptSnapshot,
116	        version: string,
117	        scriptKind?: ScriptKind,
118	        sourceFileOptions?: CreateSourceFileOptions | ScriptTarget,
119	    ): SourceFile;
120	
121	    getKeyForCompilationSettings(settings: CompilerOptions): DocumentRegistryBucketKey;
122	    /** @internal */
123	    getDocumentRegistryBucketKeyWithMode(key: DocumentRegistryBucketKey, mode: ResolutionMode): DocumentRegistryBucketKeyWithMode;
124	    /**
125	     * Informs the DocumentRegistry that a file is not needed any longer.
126	     *
127	     * Note: It is not allowed to call release on a SourceFile that was not acquired from
128	     * this registry originally.
129	     *
130	     * @param fileName The name of the file to be released
131	     * @param compilationSettings The compilation settings used to acquire the file
132	     * @param scriptKind The script kind of the file to be released
133	     *
134	     * @deprecated pass scriptKind and impliedNodeFormat for correctness
135	     */
136	    releaseDocument(fileName: string, compilationSettings: CompilerOptions, scriptKind?: ScriptKind): void;
137	    /**
138	     * Informs the DocumentRegistry that a file is not needed any longer.
139	     *
140	     * Note: It is not allowed to call release on a SourceFile that was not acquired from
141	     * this registry originally.
142	     *
143	     * @param fileName The name of the file to be released
144	     * @param compilationSettings The compilation settings used to acquire the file
145	     * @param scriptKind The script kind of the file to be released
146	     * @param impliedNodeFormat The implied source file format of the file to be released
147	     */
148	    releaseDocument(fileName: string, compilationSettings: CompilerOptions, scriptKind: ScriptKind, impliedNodeFormat: ResolutionMode): void; // eslint-disable-line @typescript-eslint/unified-signatures
149	    /**
150	     * @deprecated pass scriptKind for and impliedNodeFormat correctness */

669	function createSingleLineStringWriter(): EmitTextWriter {
670	    // Why var? It avoids TDZ checks in the runtime which can be costly.
671	    // See: https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/issues/52924
672	    /* eslint-disable no-var */
673	    var str = "";
674	    /* eslint-enable no-var */
675	    const writeText: (text: string) => void = text => str += text;
676	    return {
677	        getText: () => str,
678	        write: writeText,
679	        rawWrite: writeText,
680	        writeKeyword: writeText,
681	        writeOperator: writeText,
682	        writePunctuation: writeText,
683	        writeSpace: writeText,
684	        writeStringLiteral: writeText,
685	        writeLiteral: writeText,
686	        writeParameter: writeText,
687	        writeProperty: writeText,
688	        writeSymbol: (s, _) => writeText(s),
689	        writeTrailingSemicolon: writeText,
690	        writeComment: writeText,
691	        getTextPos: () => str.length,
692	        getLine: () => 0,
693	        getColumn: () => 0,
694	        getIndent: () => 0,
695	        isAtStartOfLine: () => false,
696	        hasTrailingComment: () => false,
697	        hasTrailingWhitespace: () => !!str.length && isWhiteSpaceLike(str.charCodeAt(str.length - 1)),
698	
699	        // Completely ignore indentation for string writers.  And map newlines to
700	        // a single space.
701	        writeLine: () => str += " ",
702	        increaseIndent: noop,
703	        decreaseIndent: noop,
704	        clear: () => str = "",
705	    };
706	}

729	export function forEachAncestor<T>(node: Node, callback: (n: Node) => T | undefined | "quit"): T | undefined {
730	    while (true) {
731	        const res = callback(node);
732	        if (res === "quit") return undefined;

739	/**
740	 * Calls `callback` for each entry in the map, returning the first truthy result.
741	 * Use `map.forEach` instead for normal iteration.
742	 *
743	 * @internal
744	 */
745	export function forEachEntry<K, V, U>(map: ReadonlyMap<K, V>, callback: (value: V, key: K) => U | undefined): U | undefined {
746	    const iterator = map.entries();
747	    for (const [key, value] of iterator) {
748	        const result = callback(value, key);
749	        if (result) {
750	            return result;
751	        }
752	    }
753	    return undefined;
754	}

756	/**
757	 * `forEachEntry` for just keys.
758	 *
759	 * @internal
760	 */
761	export function forEachKey<K, T>(map: ReadonlyCollection<K>, callback: (key: K) => T | undefined): T | undefined {
762	    const iterator = map.keys();
763	    for (const key of iterator) {
764	        const result = callback(key);
765	        if (result) {
766	            return result;
767	        }
768	    }
769	    return undefined;
770	}

928	/** @internal */
929	export function hasChangesInResolutions<K, V>(
930	    names: readonly K[],
931	    newResolutions: readonly V[],
932	    getOldResolution: (name: K) => V | undefined,
933	    comparer: (oldResolution: V, newResolution: V) => boolean,
934	): boolean {
935	    Debug.assert(names.length === newResolutions.length);
936	
937	    for (let i = 0; i < names.length; i++) {
938	        const newResolution = newResolutions[i];
939	        const entry = names[i];
940	        const oldResolution = getOldResolution(entry);
941	        const changed = oldResolution
942	            ? !newResolution || !comparer(oldResolution, newResolution)
943	            : newResolution;
944	        if (changed) {
945	            return true;
946	        }
947	    }
948	    return false;
949	}

951	// Returns true if this node contains a parse error anywhere underneath it.
952	/** @internal */
953	export function containsParseError(node: Node): boolean {
954	    aggregateChildData(node);
955	    return (node.flags & NodeFlags.ThisNodeOrAnySubNodesHasError) !== 0;
956	}
957	
958	function aggregateChildData(node: Node): void {
959	    if (!(node.flags & NodeFlags.HasAggregatedChildData)) {
960	        // A node is considered to contain a parse error if:
961	        //  a) the parser explicitly marked that it had an error
962	        //  b) any of it's children reported that it had an error.
963	        const thisNodeOrAnySubNodesHasError = ((node.flags & NodeFlags.ThisNodeHasError) !== 0) ||
964	            forEachChild(node, containsParseError);
965	
966	        // If so, mark ourselves accordingly.
967	        if (thisNodeOrAnySubNodesHasError) {
968	            (node as Mutable<Node>).flags |= NodeFlags.ThisNodeOrAnySubNodesHasError;
969	        }
970	
971	        // Also mark that we've propagated the child information to this node.  This way we can
972	        // always consult the bit directly on this node without needing to check its children
973	        // again.
974	        (node as Mutable<Node>).flags |= NodeFlags.HasAggregatedChildData;
975	    }
976	}

1027	/** @internal */
1028	export function getEndLinePosition(line: number, sourceFile: SourceFileLike): number {
1029	    Debug.assert(line >= 0);
1030	    const lineStarts = getLineStarts(sourceFile);
1031	
1032	    const lineIndex = line;
1033	    const sourceText = sourceFile.text;
1034	    if (lineIndex + 1 === lineStarts.length) {
1035	        // last line - return EOF
1036	        return sourceText.length - 1;
1037	    }
1038	    else {
1039	        // current line start
1040	        const start = lineStarts[lineIndex];
1041	        // take the start position of the next line - 1 = it should be some line break
1042	        let pos = lineStarts[lineIndex + 1] - 1;
1043	        Debug.assert(isLineBreak(sourceText.charCodeAt(pos)));
1044	        // walk backwards skipping line breaks, stop the the beginning of current line.
1045	        // i.e:
1046	        // <some text>
1047	        // $ <- end of line for this position should match the start position
1048	        while (start <= pos && isLineBreak(sourceText.charCodeAt(pos))) {
1049	            pos--;
1050	        }
1051	        return pos;
1052	    }
1053	}

1065	// Returns true if this node is missing from the actual source code. A 'missing' node is different
1066	// from 'undefined/defined'. When a node is undefined (which can happen for optional nodes
1067	// in the tree), it is definitely missing. However, a node may be defined, but still be
1068	// missing.  This happens whenever the parser knows it needs to parse something, but can't
1069	// get anything in the source code that it expects at that location. For example:
1070	//
1071	//          let a: ;
1072	//
1073	// Here, the Type in the Type-Annotation is not-optional (as there is a colon in the source
1074	// code). So the parser will attempt to parse out a type, and will create an actual node.
1075	// However, this node will be 'missing' in the sense that no actual source-code/tokens are
1076	// contained within it.
1077	/** @internal */
1078	export function nodeIsMissing(node: Node | undefined): boolean {
1079	    if (node === undefined) {
1080	        return true;
1081	    }
1082	
1083	    return node.pos === node.end && node.pos >= 0 && node.kind !== SyntaxKind.EndOfFileToken;
1084	}

1086	/** @internal */
1087	export function nodeIsPresent(node: Node | undefined): boolean {
1088	    return !nodeIsMissing(node);
1089	}

1091	/**
1092	 * Tests whether `child` is a grammar error on `parent`.
1093	 * @internal
1094	 */
1095	export function isGrammarError(parent: Node, child: Node | NodeArray<Node>): boolean {
1096	    if (isTypeParameterDeclaration(parent)) return child === parent.expression;
1097	    if (isClassStaticBlockDeclaration(parent)) return child === parent.modifiers;
1098	    if (isPropertySignature(parent)) return child === parent.initializer;
1099	    if (isPropertyDeclaration(parent)) return child === parent.questionToken && isAutoAccessorPropertyDeclaration(parent);
1100	    if (isPropertyAssignment(parent)) return child === parent.modifiers || child === parent.questionToken || child === parent.exclamationToken || isGrammarErrorElement(parent.modifiers, child, isModifierLike);
1101	    if (isShorthandPropertyAssignment(parent)) return child === parent.equalsToken || child === parent.modifiers || child === parent.questionToken || child === parent.exclamationToken || isGrammarErrorElement(parent.modifiers, child, isModifierLike);
1102	    if (isMethodDeclaration(parent)) return child === parent.exclamationToken;
1103	    if (isConstructorDeclaration(parent)) return child === parent.typeParameters || child === parent.type || isGrammarErrorElement(parent.typeParameters, child, isTypeParameterDeclaration);
1104	    if (isGetAccessorDeclaration(parent)) return child === parent.typeParameters || isGrammarErrorElement(parent.typeParameters, child, isTypeParameterDeclaration);
1105	    if (isSetAccessorDeclaration(parent)) return child === parent.typeParameters || child === parent.type || isGrammarErrorElement(parent.typeParameters, child, isTypeParameterDeclaration);
1106	    if (isNamespaceExportDeclaration(parent)) return child === parent.modifiers || isGrammarErrorElement(parent.modifiers, child, isModifierLike);
1107	    return false;
1108	}

1110	function isGrammarErrorElement<T extends Node>(nodeArray: NodeArray<T> | undefined, child: Node | NodeArray<Node>, isElement: (node: Node) => node is T) {

1112	    return contains(nodeArray, child);

1115	function insertStatementsAfterPrologue<T extends Statement>(to: T[], from: readonly T[] | undefined, isPrologueDirective: (node: Node) => boolean): T[] {
1116	    if (from === undefined || from.length === 0) return to;
1117	    let statementIndex = 0;
1118	    // skip all prologue directives to insert at the correct position
1119	    for (; statementIndex < to.length; ++statementIndex) {
1120	        if (!isPrologueDirective(to[statementIndex])) {
1121	            break;
1122	        }
1123	    }
1124	    to.splice(statementIndex, 0, ...from);
1125	    return to;
1126	}
1127	
1128	function insertStatementAfterPrologue<T extends Statement>(to: T[], statement: T | undefined, isPrologueDirective: (node: Node) => boolean): T[] {
1129	    if (statement === undefined) return to;
1130	    let statementIndex = 0;
1131	    // skip all prologue directives to insert at the correct position
1132	    for (; statementIndex < to.length; ++statementIndex) {
1133	        if (!isPrologueDirective(to[statementIndex])) {
1134	            break;
1135	        }
1136	    }
1137	    to.splice(statementIndex, 0, statement);
1138	    return to;
1139	}

1141	function isAnyPrologueDirective(node: Node) {
1142	    return isPrologueDirective(node) || !!(getEmitFlags(node) & EmitFlags.CustomPrologue);

1174	/**
1175	 * Determine if the given comment is a triple-slash
1176	 *
1177	 * @return true if the comment is a triple-slash comment else false
1178	 *
1179	 * @internal
1180	 */
1181	export function isRecognizedTripleSlashComment(text: string, commentPos: number, commentEnd: number): boolean {
1182	    // Verify this is /// comment, but do the regexp match only when we first can find /// in the comment text
1183	    // so that we don't end up computing comment string and doing match for all // comments
1184	    if (
1185	        text.charCodeAt(commentPos + 1) === CharacterCodes.slash &&
1186	        commentPos + 2 < commentEnd &&
1187	        text.charCodeAt(commentPos + 2) === CharacterCodes.slash
1188	    ) {
1189	        const textSubStr = text.substring(commentPos, commentEnd);
1190	        return fullTripleSlashReferencePathRegEx.test(textSubStr) ||
1191	                fullTripleSlashAMDReferencePathRegEx.test(textSubStr) ||
1192	                fullTripleSlashAMDModuleRegEx.test(textSubStr) ||
1193	                fullTripleSlashReferenceTypeReferenceDirectiveRegEx.test(textSubStr) ||
1194	                fullTripleSlashLibReferenceRegEx.test(textSubStr) ||
1195	                defaultLibReferenceRegEx.test(textSubStr) ?
1196	            true : false;
1197	    }
1198	    return false;
1199	}
1200	
1201	/** @internal */
1202	export function isPinnedComment(text: string, start: number): boolean {
1203	    return text.charCodeAt(start + 1) === CharacterCodes.asterisk &&
1204	        text.charCodeAt(start + 2) === CharacterCodes.exclamation;
1205	}

1207	/** @internal */
1208	export function createCommentDirectivesMap(sourceFile: SourceFile, commentDirectives: CommentDirective[]): CommentDirectivesMap {
1209	    const directivesByLine = new Map(
1210	        commentDirectives.map(commentDirective => [
1211	            `${getLineAndCharacterOfPosition(sourceFile, commentDirective.range.end).line}`,
1212	            commentDirective,
1213	        ]),
1214	    );
1215	
1216	    const usedLines = new Map<string, boolean>();
1217	
1218	    return { getUnusedExpectations, markUsed };
1219	
1220	    function getUnusedExpectations() {
1221	        return arrayFrom(directivesByLine.entries())
1222	            .filter(([line, directive]) => directive.type === CommentDirectiveType.ExpectError && !usedLines.get(line))
1223	            .map(([_, directive]) => directive);
1224	    }
1225	
1226	    function markUsed(line: number) {
1227	        if (!directivesByLine.has(`${line}`)) {
1228	            return false;
1229	        }
1230	
1231	        usedLines.set(`${line}`, true);
1232	        return true;
1233	    }
1234	}

1236	/** @internal */
1237	export function getTokenPosOfNode(node: Node, sourceFile?: SourceFileLike, includeJsDoc?: boolean): number {
1238	    // With nodes that have no width (i.e. 'Missing' nodes), we actually *don't*
1239	    // want to skip trivia because this will launch us forward to the next token.
1240	    if (nodeIsMissing(node)) {
1241	        return node.pos;
1242	    }
1243	
1244	    if (isJSDocNode(node) || node.kind === SyntaxKind.JsxText) {
1245	        // JsxText cannot actually contain comments, even though the scanner will think it sees comments
1246	        return skipTrivia((sourceFile ?? getSourceFileOfNode(node)).text, node.pos, /*stopAfterLineBreak*/ false, /*stopAtComments*/ true);
1247	    }
1248	
1249	    if (includeJsDoc && hasJSDocNodes(node)) {
1250	        return getTokenPosOfNode(node.jsDoc![0], sourceFile);
1251	    }
1252	
1253	    // For a syntax list, it is possible that one of its children has JSDocComment nodes, while
1254	    // the syntax list itself considers them as normal trivia. Therefore if we simply skip
1255	    // trivia for the list, we may have skipped the JSDocComment as well. So we should process its
1256	    // first child to determine the actual position of its first token.
1257	    if (node.kind === SyntaxKind.SyntaxList) {
1258	        sourceFile ??= getSourceFileOfNode(node);
1259	        const first = firstOrUndefined(getNodeChildren(node, sourceFile));
1260	        if (first) {
1261	            return getTokenPosOfNode(first, sourceFile, includeJsDoc);
1262	        }
1263	    }
1264	
1265	    return skipTrivia(
1266	        (sourceFile ?? getSourceFileOfNode(node)).text,
1267	        node.pos,
1268	        /*stopAfterLineBreak*/ false,
1269	        /*stopAtComments*/ false,
1270	        isInJSDoc(node),
1271	    );
1272	}

1274	/** @internal */
1275	export function getNonDecoratorTokenPosOfNode(node: Node, sourceFile?: SourceFileLike): number {
1276	    const lastDecorator = !nodeIsMissing(node) && canHaveModifiers(node) ? findLast(node.modifiers, isDecorator) : undefined;
1277	    if (!lastDecorator) {
1278	        return getTokenPosOfNode(node, sourceFile);
1279	    }
1280	
1281	    return skipTrivia((sourceFile || getSourceFileOfNode(node)).text, lastDecorator.end);
1282	}

1304	export function moduleExportNameTextEscaped(node: ModuleExportName): __String {
1305	    return node.kind === SyntaxKind.StringLiteral ? escapeLeadingUnderscores(node.text) : node.escapedText;

1308	/**
1309	 * Equality checks against a keyword without underscores don't need to bother
1310	 * to turn "__" into "___" or vice versa, since they will never be equal in
1311	 * either case. So we can ignore those cases to improve performance.

1316	    return (node.kind === SyntaxKind.StringLiteral ? node.text : node.escapedText) === InternalSymbolName.Default;

1319	/** @internal */
1320	export function getTextOfNodeFromSourceText(sourceText: string, node: Node, includeTrivia = false): string {
1321	    if (nodeIsMissing(node)) {
1322	        return "";
1323	    }
1324	
1325	    let text = sourceText.substring(includeTrivia ? node.pos : skipTrivia(sourceText, node.pos), node.end);

1329	        text = text.split(/\r\n|\n|\r/).map(line => line.replace(/^\s*\*/, "").trimStart()).join("\n");
1330	    }
1331	
1332	    return text;
1333	}
